isolation.scope: Fresh isolated browser session; only in-browser game state was mutated.

assertions.title: Drag-and-Drop Movement
assertions.reason: Dragging the e2 pawn to e4 moved it to the destination and cleared e2; dragging the queen from h5 to e5 captured the pawn on e5; live drag feedback showed a lifted drag piece with drop shadow while the origin piece was hidden.

observations: DOM check after drag move: white pawn present on e4 and absent from e2.
DOM check after drag capture: white queen present on e5, black pawn removed from e5.
Live drag inspection: dragPieceExists=true, originOpacity=0, dragFilter included drop-shadow.

assertions.title: Click-to-Move
assertions.reason: Clicking the g1 knight selected its square, clicking it again deselected it, clicking the d1 queen reselected the active piece, and clicking h5 moved the queen from d1 to h5.

observations: Selected square box shadow appeared on g1 and cleared after clicking the same piece again.
Selection transferred from g1 to d1 when the queen was clicked.
DOM check after move: white queen present on h5 and absent from d1.

assertions.title: Legal Move Highlights
assertions.reason: Selecting the g1 knight showed the expected L-shaped targets on e2, f3, and h3; selecting the queen on h5 showed distinct capture-ring indicators on capturable squares and dot indicators on quiet moves; indicators cleared on deselect.

observations: Knight indicator squares: square e2, square f3, square h3.
Queen selection showed captureIndicator markup on e5 and f7, and moveIndicator markup on g4 and h3.
Indicator list was empty after deselecting the piece.

assertions.title: Illegal Move Rejection
assertions.reason: The UI would not move Black's bishop on White's turn, and in the pinned-knight position after a3 d5 a4 e5 d3 Bb4+ Nc3 d4, dragging the pinned knight from c3 to b5 was rejected and the knight remained on c3.

observations: Attempting to click square b4 then square a5 left the black bishop on b4 and did not select an opponent piece.
After the illegal drag attempt, white knight remained on c3 and did not appear on b5.
Pinned-piece scenario was verified with black bishop on b4 and black pawn on d4 before the illegal drag.

assertions.title: Last Move Highlighting
assertions.reason: Before White's next move, the previous black move highlighted d5 and d4; after White played a4-a5, the highlight updated to a4 and a5 and the d-file highlight was removed.

observations: Before update, highlighted squares included square d5 and square d4.
After White's move, highlighted squares were square a4 and square a5 only.

assertions.title: Check Indicator
assertions.reason: After a3 d5 a4 e5 d3 Bb4+, the e1 king square showed the alarm-style red highlight and the status text reported 'Your king is in check.'; after resolving with Nc3, the e1 highlight cleared.

observations: Check-state CSS on e1 included the red squareCheck inset box-shadow.
After Nc3, DOM check showed white knight on c3, black pawn on d4, status returned to 'Your move.', and e1 box shadow was none.

frictions.description: agent-browser's drag helper did not accept @ref selectors in this session, so drag verification used CSS selectors for committed moves and low-level mouse events for the live drag-feedback capture.
frictions.resolution: Used CSS-selector drag for move/capture assertions and re-ran a mouse-driven drag after scrolling the board back into view to capture the lift-effect evidence.

summary: Tested 6 assigned board-interaction assertions; all 6 passed with screenshot evidence and no console/runtime errors observed.
